extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each entity: I excluded all “By our Company,” “By our Promoter,” “By our Directors,” and “By our KMPs/members of Senior Management” rows, and counted only the against rows. Criminal proceedings against comprise 0 for the Company, 1 for the Promoter, 1 for Directors and 0 for KMPs/Senior Management; statutory/regulatory proceedings against are nil, while tax proceedings against the Company are 1; the table lists no subsidiaries. The quantified amount against the Company is ₹23.29 million for tax proceedings, with no quantified against amount for Promoters, Directors or KMPs, giving ₹23.29 million = ₹2.329 crore; the source unit is million. Contingent liabilities use the stated Total row of ₹23.29 million as at March 31, 2025, converted to ₹2.329 crore, rather than summing line items. The customer concentration percentages are company-wide but are stated as a percentage of Contract Price, not revenue from operations or total income, so the top-five and top-ten fields are not reported under the required denominator; the statutory auditor’s report explicitly says the opinion is not modified and contains an emphasis of matter only.
